text.title: multi-agent-production-bus Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for multi-agent-production-bus. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: All inter-agent messages MUST use structured content (JSON-serializable dict or validated schema). Free-form string messages MUST NOT be sent via the bus in production.
rule.rationale: Free-form text increases parsing failures at the receiving agent. Structured messages allow schema validation before dispatch and make the message_history machine-readable for cost attribution and replay debugging.

rule.statement: Every agent handler MUST be wrapped with asyncio.wait_for(handler(message), timeout=X). Never await a handler without a timeout in a production bus.
rule.rationale: A single slow or hung agent handler blocks the entire send() coroutine. Without a timeout, one agent can deadlock the bus. Set timeouts at the bus level so individual agent failures degrade gracefully.
